CheckTitle: Linode Instance with public IP has a Cloud Firewall attached
Description: Linode instances with public IPv4 addresses are assessed for Cloud Firewall attachment. Publicly accessible instances without a firewall have no network-level access controls, making them directly vulnerable to internet-based attacks.
Risk: A publicly accessible instance without a firewall allows unrestricted inbound traffic from the internet. All running services are exposed to port scanning, brute-force attacks, and exploitation of vulnerabilities.

Code.CLI: linode-cli firewalls create --label my-firewall && linode-cli firewalls device-create <firewall_id> --id <linode_id> --type linode
Code.Other:
1. Log in to the Linode Cloud Manager
2. Navigate to the Firewalls section
3. Create a new firewall with restrictive inbound rules
4. Add the instance as a device to the firewall

Recommendation.Text: Attach a Cloud Firewall to this publicly accessible instance. Configure rules to allow only necessary inbound traffic (e.g., HTTP/HTTPS) and deny all other ports.
